dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, we lost people in the last attack. Good people. And the ones who survived are falling apart.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} raid took lives: a guard, a worker, an elder who refused to abandon their post. We drove them off, but the victory feels hollow. {target_npc} hasn't spoken since the burial. Half the settlement is talking about abandoning this place. I need someone to retrieve {quest_item} from {location_hint} so we can build a proper remembrance for the fallen.
dialogueChunks.outro: If we don't give people something to rally around, grief will destroy what the raid couldn't. {quest_stakes_The} depends on it. {quest_reward_hint} is the shared strength we need.

dialogueChunks.intro: My partner was killed on the road two weeks ago, {player_name}. I've made my peace with that. But there's one thing I can't let go of.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They were carrying {quest_item} when the {enemy_type_plural} ambushed the caravan {location_hint}. It was a gift they'd made for our child: the only thing our child would have to remember them by. The caravan was looted and the spoils were carried off. {target_npc} says the {enemy_type_plural} probably took it to their camp.
dialogueChunks.outro: I'm not asking you to avenge them. I'm asking you to {quest_action} and bring home the one thing my child will hold when asking about their parent. {quest_stakes_The} {quest_stakes_is} everything to us. {quest_threat_The} {quest_threat_is} the finality of it.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I should have died instead. I know that's not a productive thought, but it's the only one I have most days.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My partner and I were building something together at the edge of the settlement. They were out near {quest_focus_the} when the {enemy_type_plural} found them. I was supposed to go that day, but I stayed behind because I was tired. Tired. That's the reason I'm alive and they're not.
dialogueChunks.outro: {target_npc} has been trying to get me to function. But the only thing that helps is the thought of finishing what we started. I need you to {quest_action} and retrieve {quest_item} from {location_hint}: the materials they never got to bring home. {quest_stakes_The} gives me a reason to keep going. {quest_reward_hint} would be a purpose renewed.
